paragraphs.text: Greetings European Commission Representatives. I would like to give my personal view to the digital fairness act. As a individual person that plays video games on a dialy basis and has a passion for videogames. I fear the life of video games comes to a end if develepors/publisers keep up this "no end of life plan", and the shut down services of the videogames. Games like: The Crew, Firefall, Defiance 2050, Elite Dangerous: Arena, these games are dead, they cant be played, cant be acsessed. If this practice continues of destroying what people paid for, who want to buy games from the publishers/developers when people fear the next game on theyr list might be taken away from them. This will not only hurt the players, but it will hurt the developers/publishers due to people dont want to buy from developers that is known for destroying what you paid for. If Publishers/Developers had some sort of end of life plan to let people enjoy the game even after the official servers shut down. a few exsaples... 1: release server files so peopel can host theyr own private servers. 2: release the server code, so the gaming comunity can update and patch problems as buggs apear, and/or even add features that can improove on the game server itself. 3: single player games would only need a change in the code to enable local savegame storage these options would allow games still be playable and enjoyable even after Developers/Åublishers shuts the online service down And people would feel more safer that way knowing what they paid for is not stolen away from them. Why does museums preserve and restore artifacts, art, documents, etc? Its to preserve what once was, so people now, and even the next generation can do and see said items. Videogames is just as much art as what you find in a museum. And you could ask the same about videogames, why don't developers/publishes have a end of life plan, so people can play said games even after a server is shut down. And if they had such a end of life plan, the next generation to see AND experience what people in the 80's 90's 20s played. Digital games, is more than pixels on the sceen. Some game are therapy for people. Some games require people to problem solve. Even games can teach other languages even tohugh people are not aware of it, ( I learned about 80% of english via PC games) Games have rich story, a exsamle is the Mass Effect Universe, Doom Lore, Borderlands Universe, Monkey Island. Amazing games that gives countless of hours, even the option to choose for yourself, a interactive book you live in. If killing games is not stoped, what will be next? digital books, movies, music? sorry, but all the movies you bought on said platform, is going away, thanks for the money though... byeeee! And you are left with nothing, not a option to preserve said items What I ask for is.. Preserve what we paid for, we all need this.
